hero.title: Find the rack architecture that fits the room, the load, and the growth curve.
hero.subtitle: Answer five focused questions and move from abstract requirements to a Bodea recommendation set that feels deliberate, premium, and ready for purchase.
hero.body: This experience weighs deployment context, rack density, noise posture, cabling pressure, and growth expectations to surface the best-fit collection first and the right adjacent categories second.

personas.title: Compact Edge
personas.description: For branch offices, satellite closets, and right-sized environments where you want disciplined capacity without overbuilding the room.
personas.rationale: You leaned toward smaller rack-unit counts, practical deployment settings, and a buying posture that values a compact but polished install.

collection.title: Compact edge shortlist
collection.description: A focused set of Bodea enclosures for smaller environments that still need clean airflow, cable discipline, and a premium finish.

personas.title: Quiet Office Retail
personas.description: For open offices, retail backrooms, and polished commercial spaces where noise, visual restraint, and approachable servicing matter.
personas.rationale: Your answers favored quieter operation, customer-adjacent deployment, and a rack profile that feels refined rather than industrial.

collection.title: Quiet office shortlist
collection.description: Server-rack recommendations for spaces where acoustics, polish, and moderate growth all matter at once.

personas.title: Secure Growth
personas.description: For teams planning ahead: enough room to grow, stronger access control, and a cabinet posture that stays professional as density rises.
personas.rationale: You signaled balanced-to-medium rack heights, controlled access, and a buying posture that expects additional equipment in the near term.

collection.title: Secure growth shortlist
collection.description: A curated set for teams that need stronger access control today and headroom for tomorrow's network expansion.

personas.title: High Capacity Core
personas.description: For server rooms and primary network cores where capacity, airflow, and disciplined cable management take priority over a smaller footprint.
personas.rationale: Your answers emphasized larger rack-unit ranges, higher density, and more ambitious scaling expectations, which points to a core-room strategy.

collection.title: High-capacity shortlist
collection.description: A denser recommendation path for primary rooms, core closets, and service-heavy deployments that need real capacity headroom.

personas.title: Industrial Edge
personas.description: For harsher operational environments with heavier cable churn, faster servicing, and a more utilitarian infrastructure posture.
personas.rationale: You favored warehouse or industrial deployment, heavier service needs, and an environment that benefits from more operational resilience.
strengths: Pairs naturally with cooling and cable-management add-ons

collection.title: Industrial edge shortlist
collection.description: A tougher Bodea recommendation path for warehouse edge, utility spaces, and environments with frequent operational change.

questions.title: Where will this rack or enclosure live?
questions.description: The room changes the answer. Acoustic expectations, service posture, and physical footprint all start here.
questions.supportCopy: Choose the environment that is closest to the real install, not the ideal one.

answers.label: Branch office or satellite closet
answers.subtitle: Tight footprint, practical service access, polished enough for shared spaces.

answers.label: Open office or retail backroom
answers.subtitle: Customer-adjacent, acoustically aware, and visually restrained.

answers.label: Dedicated server room
answers.subtitle: Higher density, stronger airflow expectations, and more growth headroom.

answers.label: Warehouse or industrial edge
answers.subtitle: Operationally busy, more utilitarian, and often more demanding on service access.

questions.title: What rack capacity feels right today?
questions.description: We are using current demand as the anchor, then letting growth expectations shape the alternates.
questions.supportCopy: If you are between sizes, choose the higher band only if you already expect expansion in the next cycle.

answers.label: 1-6U compact edge
answers.subtitle: Smaller switch stacks, branch-office kits, or edge utility gear.

answers.label: 7-12U balanced growth
answers.subtitle: A practical sweet spot for mixed network stacks and more polished commercial deployments.

answers.label: 13-24U mid-scale core
answers.subtitle: More room for denser cabling, security posture, and stronger adjacent thermal planning.

answers.label: 25-42U high capacity
answers.subtitle: Primary rooms, denser infrastructure, and the clearest fit for thermal and cable-management add-ons.

questions.title: What matters most in the buying decision?
questions.description: This becomes the strongest tie-breaker inside the persona group that already fits your capacity and environment.
questions.supportCopy: Pick the single thing that would disappoint you most if the rack got it wrong.

answers.label: Quiet operation
answers.subtitle: Lower perceived noise and a more refined feel for visible or adjacent spaces.

answers.label: Security and lockable access
answers.subtitle: Controlled servicing, better access posture, and stronger day-to-day governance.

answers.label: Airflow and thermal headroom
answers.subtitle: A stronger fit for warmer rooms, denser infrastructure, and future accessory stacking.

answers.label: Fast service access
answers.subtitle: Fewer obstacles when the rack changes often or technicians need quick access windows.

questions.title: How dense or change-heavy is the cabling?
questions.description: Cable pressure influences whether we should bias toward a cleaner footprint, denser core posture, or heavier adjacent cable-management emphasis.
questions.supportCopy: Think in terms of how often the routing changes, not only how many cables you have today.

answers.label: Light and stable
answers.subtitle: A few devices, limited churn, and a cleaner install rhythm.

answers.label: Moderate with occasional changes
answers.subtitle: A balanced mid-range condition that still benefits from disciplined cable management.

answers.label: Dense and actively changing
answers.subtitle: More service windows, more routing pressure, and a stronger argument for higher-capacity posture.

answers.label: Very dense or highly variable
answers.subtitle: A living infrastructure environment where routing, access, and add-on planning matter continuously.

questions.title: How quickly do you expect this environment to grow?
questions.description: The final step decides whether we should keep the recommendation disciplined, stretch it for growth, or bias toward a more operationally resilient path.
questions.supportCopy: Use the next six to twelve months as the planning window.

answers.label: Stable for the next year
answers.subtitle: The deployment is known, relatively settled, and not expected to surge quickly.

answers.label: Moderate growth in six to twelve months
answers.subtitle: Enough change is coming that a little more room today may protect the next buying cycle.

answers.label: Aggressive growth in the near term
answers.subtitle: Capacity, cable pressure, and adjacent thermal planning are all likely to intensify quickly.

answers.label: Unknown or operationally unpredictable
answers.subtitle: A changing environment with unclear next requirements, where resilience matters as much as elegance.

crossCategoryModules.title: Server Racks
crossCategoryModules.description: Explore the full server-rack catalog if you want to widen the shortlist beyond the recommended persona.

crossCategoryModules.title: Network Enclosures
crossCategoryModules.description: A natural adjacent category when the deployment leans compact, secure, or branch-oriented.

crossCategoryModules.title: Power and Cooling
crossCategoryModules.description: Thermal and power accessories become more important as density and growth expectations rise.

crossCategoryModules.title: Cable Management
crossCategoryModules.description: A higher-value add-on when service access and change-heavy routing are central to the environment.

crossCategoryModules.title: Accessories
crossCategoryModules.description: Round out the solution with the finishing layer that keeps the rack disciplined once it is live.
